---
title: "02 ‚Äî Spatial frames"
subtitle: "`NeuroSpace` is a value object. `compatible_with` is *the* alignment check."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: start
---

A neuroimaging array without its spatial frame is half a value. Many fMRI mistakes come from carrying the array without the frame and noticing too late. `NeuroSpace` is the part the rest of the API uses to decide whether two arrays mean the same thing.

## What it is

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "start" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

bold.space.dim, bold.space.spacing.tolist(), bold.space.origin.tolist()
```

`NeuroSpace` is **frozen**. Its `dim`, `spacing`, `origin`, and 4√ó4 `trans` (affine) are set at construction and never mutate. Two `NeuroSpace`s with the same dim, spacing, origin, and affine compare equal and produce the same `hash_neurospace` digest.

That immutability is what makes content-addressable provenance work: a hashed space pinned in a `Receipt` continues to identify the same frame for the life of the process and the file.

## What it guarantees

```{python}
bold.space.compatible_with(mask.space)
```

`compatible_with` is the alignment check. It returns `True` when two spaces describe the same grid (first three dims) with the same affine within `atol=1e-6`. It does **not** return `False` on a mismatch ‚Äî it raises `ValueError` naming the offending field:

```{python}
#| error: true
from neuroim.neuro_space import NeuroSpace

shifted = NeuroSpace(tuple(mask.space.dim), spacing=(9, 9, 9))
mask.space.compatible_with(shifted)   # raises ValueError: "NeuroSpace mismatch in affine: ..."
```

A true/raise predicate (rather than true/false) is deliberate: the failure carries the mismatched field and both values, so a mask/space mistake is diagnosable from the traceback alone instead of from a silent `False` the caller forgot to check. You rarely call `compatible_with` yourself ‚Äî but every public operation that takes a `mask=` argument routes through `verify.assert_same_space`, which calls it, before it touches the array.

## Where the affine comes from

`bold.space.trans` is the 4√ó4 voxel-to-world affine. It is the same matrix `nib.load(...).affine` returns for the same file. The grid-to-world transform is *one* of the four fields hashed into `Receipt.input_space_hash`, and the only one hashed alone into `Receipt.source_affine_hash`. Two spaces with the same dim/spacing/origin but a different affine produce different hashes; the contract layer treats them as distinct frames.

## What it doesn't do

- **No units of time.** `NeuroSpace` carries the time axis as a dimension but does not record the repetition time. TR is data, not contract.
- **No coordinate conversion overload.** Use `apply_affine(space.trans, [x, y, z])` for grid‚Üíworld and the inverse for world‚Üígrid. There is no implicit conversion when comparing spaces.
- **No mutation.** `dim`, `spacing`, `origin`, `trans` are read-only arrays. Code that needs a modified frame constructs a new `NeuroSpace`; the original keeps its identity for the receipts that reference it.

## Refuses to

- **`compatible_with` on differently-shaped grids or affines.** Raises `ValueError`: `"NeuroSpace mismatch in spatial dim: left=‚Ä¶, right=‚Ä¶"` or `"NeuroSpace mismatch in affine: left=‚Ä¶, right=‚Ä¶"`. Public operations wrap this as `"assert_same_space: spatial contract mismatch ‚Äî ‚Ä¶"`.
- **Construction with non-positive dims or spacing.** `NeuroSpace((-1, 4, 4))` raises `ValueError: "All dimensions must be positive"`; bad spacing raises `"Spacing values must be positive"`.
- **Mutation.** Assigning to `dim`, `spacing`, `origin`, or `trans` raises `AttributeError: "NeuroSpace is immutable"`.
- **Wrongly-shaped `trans=`.** A 3-D space with a non-4√ó4 affine raises `"trans must be 4x4 matrix for 3D space, got ‚Ä¶"`; a non-invertible affine raises `"Transformation matrix must be invertible"`.

## Next

[03 ‚Äî ROI extraction](03-roi.qmd) is the first page where a `compatible_with` check fires inside a public operation, and the first page where the return is a `Result` carrying a `Receipt`.

## See also

- [Concept: NeuroSpace](../concepts/space.qmd) ‚Äî the short-form vocabulary entry.
- [Spec: Receipt v1](../spec/receipt-nifti-extension.html) ‚Äî how `space.dim/spacing/origin/trans` are hashed into a Receipt.
- [Evidence](../evidence/index.qmd) ‚Äî runnable checks that exercise spatial-frame validation across common workflows.
